title: Xyris obtusiuscula
taxonomy: Xyris obtusiuscula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Xyridaceae. It belongs to the genus Xyris, which consists of over 200 species of plants. Xyris obtusiuscula is native to North America, specifically the southeastern United States. This plant is a perennial, producing yellow flowers and growing in wetland environments. Its classification is further supported by its placement within the order Poales.

intro: Xyris obtusiuscula, commonly known as yellow-eyed grass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Xyridaceae family, native to eastern North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Xyris obtusiuscula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Xyris obtusiuscula include tea infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote skin health.
